title: Recursive CTE
content:
### Recursive CTE

A recursive CTE is a CTE that references itself. It is used for querying hierarchical or tree-structured data like organizational charts, bill of materials, or category trees.

---

### Recursive CTE Syntax

```sql
WITH RECURSIVE cte_name AS (
    -- Anchor member (base case)
    SELECT ...
    FROM ...
    WHERE ...
    UNION ALL
    -- Recursive member (references the CTE)
    SELECT ...
    FROM cte_name
    JOIN ...
)
SELECT * FROM cte_name;
```

### Organization Chart Example

```sql
WITH RECURSIVE org_chain AS (
    -- Anchor: find the CEO (no manager)
    SELECT id, name, manager_id, 1 AS level
    FROM employees
    WHERE manager_id IS NULL

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive: find direct reports
    SELECT e.id, e.name, e.manager_id, oc.level + 1
    FROM employees e
    JOIN org_chain oc ON e.manager_id = oc.id
)
SELECT * FROM org_chain ORDER BY level, name;
```

### Number Generation

```sql
-- Generate sequence of numbers (useful for calendar tables)
WITH RECURSIVE numbers(n) AS (
    SELECT 1
    UNION ALL
    SELECT n + 1 FROM numbers WHERE n < 10
)
SELECT * FROM numbers;
```

### Parts Explosion (Bill of Materials)

```sql
WITH RECURSIVE parts_tree AS (
    SELECT id, name, parent_id, 1 AS level
    FROM parts
    WHERE parent_id IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT p.id, p.name, p.parent_id, pt.level + 1
    FROM parts p
    JOIN parts_tree pt ON p.parent_id = pt.id
)
SELECT * FROM parts_tree;
```

> **Key Point:** A recursive CTE has two parts: an anchor (non-recursive starting point) and a recursive member that joins back to the CTE. The recursion stops when no new rows are produced.
codeExample:
WITH RECURSIVE employee_hierarchy AS (
    SELECT id, name, manager_id, 1 AS depth
    FROM employees WHERE manager_id IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT e.id, e.name, e.manager_id, eh.depth + 1
    FROM employees e
    JOIN employee_hierarchy eh ON e.manager_id = eh.id
)
SELECT * FROM employee_hierarchy ORDER BY depth, name;

quiz.question: What are the two parts of a recursive CTE?
When does a recursive CTE stop?
options: When no new rows are produced
